For `Book.__repr__`, use `f"Book({self.title!r}, {self.author!r}, {self.year})"`. The `!r` wraps strings in quotes automatically.
For `Book.__eq__`, compare all three attributes: `return self.title == other.title and self.author == other.author and self.year == other.year`.
For `Book.to_dict`, return a plain dict: `{"title": self.title, "author": self.author, "year": self.year}`.
For `Book.from_dict`, it's a `@classmethod` — use `cls(d["title"], d["author"], d["year"])` to build the object.
For `Library.from_dict`, create a new `Library()`, then loop over `d["books"]` and call `self.add(Book.from_dict(book_dict))` for each one.
